sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question:
State with reasons, whether the following statements are True or False:
(i) The value of closing inventory under simple average price method is realistic as compare to LIFO.

(ii) M/s Raj Yog & Co. runs a cafe. They renovated some of the old cabins. Because of this renovation some space was made free and number of cabins was increased from 25 to 28. The total expenditure incurred was Rs. 50,000 and was treated as a revenue expenditure.

(iii) A Non-Profit Organization registered under Section 8 of Companies Act, 2013 can distribute its surplus among its members.

(iv) When there is no partnership deed prevails, the interest on loan of a partner to be paid @ 6%.

(v) While preparing the bank reconciliation statement starting with debit balance as per pass book or bank statement, the deposited cheques that are not yet cleared need not be adjusted.

(vi) It is not mandatory to incorporate a company under the companies act.
sub_questions.answer:
(i) Value of Closing stock as per average method is more realistic then LIFO.

(ii) Renovation of cabins increased the number of cabins. This has an effect on the future revenue generating capability of the business. Thus, this renovation expense is capital expenditure in nature.

(iii) A Non-profit organization registered under section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013 can't distribute surplus to its members. The surplus must be credited to General fund for furtherance of its charitable objectives.

(iv) When there is no partnership deed then the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act are to be applied for settling the dispute. Interest on loan is payable @ 6% p.a. as per Indian Partnership Act.

(v) Cheques deposited but not yet cleared should be subtracted from debit or unfavourable balance in passbook.

(vi) False: It is mandatory to incorporate a company under the Companies Act. Without such incorporation, a company cannot come into existence.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question: Classify the following expenditures as capital or revenue expenditure:  (i) Amount paid for removal of stock to a new site.  (ii) Rings and Pistons of an engine were changed to get full efficiency.
sub_questions.answer: (i) Amount paid for removal of stock to a new site is treated as a Revenue Expenditure because it is not enhancing the value of any asset.  (ii) Expenditure incurred for changing Rings and Pistons of an engine is a Revenue Expenditure because, the change of rings and piston will restore the efficiency of the engine only and it will not add anything to the capacity of the engine.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question: Explain any 2 differences between Bill of Exchange and Promissory Notes.
sub_questions.answer:
Rules regarding posting of entries in the ledger:
1. Separate account is opened in ledger book for each account and entries from journal are posted to respective ledger account accordingly.
2. It is a practice to use words ‘To’ and ‘By’ while posting transactions in the ledger. The word ‘To’ is used in the particular column with the accounts written on the debit side while ‘By’ is used with the accounts written in the particular column of the credit side. These ‘To’ and ‘By’ do not have any meanings but are used to the account debited and credited.
3. The concerned account debited in the journal should also be debited in the ledger but reference should be of the respective credit account.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question: Discuss the factors taken into consideration for calculation of depreciation.
sub_questions.answer: Following factors are taken into consideration for calculation of depreciation. 1. **Cost of asset** including expenses for installation, commissioning, trial run etc.- Cost of a depreciable asset represents its money outlay or its equivalent in connection with its acquisition, installation and commissioning as well as for additions to or improvement thereof for the purpose of increase in efficiency. 2. **Estimated useful life of the asset** - Useful Life’ is either (i) the period over which a depreciable asset is expected to be used by the enterprise or (ii) the number of production or similar units expected to be obtained from the use of the asset by the enterprise. Determination of the useful life is a matter of estimation and is normally based on various factors including experience with similar type of assets. Several other factors like estimated working hours, production capacity, repairs and renewals, etc. are also taken into consideration on demanding situation. 3. Estimated scrap value (if any) is calculated at the end of useful life of the asset. If such value is considered as insignificant, it is normally regarded as nil. On the other hand, if the residual value is likely to be significant, it is estimated at the time of acquisition/installation, or at the time of subsequent revaluation of asset.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question: Ali draws a bill for ₹45,000 on Akbar on 15th April, 20XX for 3 months, which is returned by Akbar to Ali after accepting the same. Ali gets it discounted with the bank for ₹ 44,100 on 18th April, 20XX and remits one-third amount to Akbar. On the due date Ali fails to remit the amount due to Akbar, but he accepts bill of ₹ 52,500 for 3 months, which Akbar discounts for ₹51,300 and remits ₹ 8,475 to Ali. Pass necessary Journal entries for the above transactions in the books of Ali.
sub_questions.answer:
| Date       | Particulars                              | Debit Amount ₹ | Credit Amount ₹ |
|------------|------------------------------------------|----------------|-----------------|
| 20XX       |                                          |                |                 |
| April 15   | Bills receivable A/c                     | Dr.            | 45,000          |
|            | To Akbar’s A/c                           |                | 45,000          |
|            | (Being acceptance received from Akbar for mutual accommodation) | | |
| April 18   | Bank A/c                                 | Dr.            | 44,100          |
|            | Discount A/c                             | Dr.            | 900             |
|            | To Bills receivable A/c                  |                | 45,000          |
|            | (Being bill discounted with bank)        |                |                 |
| April 18   | Akbar’s A/c                              | Dr.            | 15,000          |
|            | To Bank A/c                              |                | 14,700          |
|            | To Discount A/c                          |                | 300             |
|            | (Being one-third proceeds of the bill sent to Akbar) | | |
| July 18    | Akbar’s A/c                              | Dr.            | 52,500          |
|            | To Bills payable A/c                     |                | 52,500          |
|            | (Being Acceptance given)                 |                |                 |
| July 18    | Bank A/c                                 | Dr.            | 8,475           |
|            | Discount A/c                             | Dr.            | 900             |
|            | {1,200 x (30,000+8,475)}                 |                |                 |
|            | 51,300                                  |                |                 |
|            | To Akbar’s A/c                          |                | 9,375           |
|            | (Being proceeds of second bill received from Akbar) | |

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question:
Arpit Ltd., with an authorized capital of **₹ 20,00,000 divided into Equity shares of ₹ 10 each, on 1st June, 20X1, invited applications for issuing 3,00,000 Equity shares at a premium of ₹ 5 per share. The amount was payable as follows:
On Application ₹ 2 per share
On Allotment (1st July, 20X1) ₹ 7 (including premium) per share
On First call (1st Nov, 20X1) ₹ 3 per share
On Final call (1st Jan, 20X2) ₹ 3 per share
All the shares were applied for and allotted. Mr. Naresh who held 20,000 shares paid the whole of the amount due on calls along with allotment money. The final call was fully paid except a shareholder having 5,000 shares who paid his due amount on 1st March, 20X2 i.e. after 2 months along with interest on calls in arrears @ 10% p.a. Company also paid interest @ 12% p.a. on calls in advance to Mr. Naresh on 1st Jan., 20X2.
Give journal entries with narrations to record all these transactions in the books of Arpit Ltd.
PS*: Please read authorized capital ₹ 20,00,000 as authorized capital 20,00,000
sub_questions.answer:
In the books of Arpit Ltd Journal Entries

| Date | Particulars | L.F. | Debit Amount (₹) | Credit Amount (₹) |
|--------|------------------------------------|------|------------------|-------------------|
| 1-6-X1 | Bank A/c Dr. | | 6,00,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Application A/c | | | 6,00,000 |
| | (Money received on applications for 3,00,000 shares @ ₹2 per share) | | | |
| 1-7-X1 | Equity Share Application A/c Dr. | | 6,00,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Capital A/c | | | 6,00,000 |
| | (Transfer of application money to share capital) | | | |
| 1-7-X1 | Equity Share Allotment A/c Dr. | | 21,00,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Capital A/c | | | 6,00,000 |
| | To Securities Premium A/c | | | 15,00,000 |
| | (Allotment due including premium) | | | |

| Date | Particulars | L.F. | Debit Amount (₹) | Credit Amount (₹) |
|----------|------------------------------------------|------|------------------|-------------------|
| 1-7-X1 | Bank A/c Dr. | | 22,20,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Allotment A/c | | | 21,00,000 |
| | To Calls in Advance A/c | | | 1,20,000 |
| | (Allotment money received with advance) | | | |
| 1-11-X1 | Equity Share First Call A/c Dr. | | 9,00,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Capital A/c | | | 9,00,000 |
| | (First call due) | | | |
| 1-11-X1 | Bank A/c Dr. | | 8,40,000 | |
| | Calls in Advance A/c Dr. | | 60,000 | |
| | To Equity Share First Call A/c | | | 9,00,000 |
| | (First call received and adjusted) | | | |
| 1-1-X2 | Equity Share Final Call A/c Dr. | | 9,00,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Capital A/c | | | 9,00,000 |
| | (Final call due) | | | |
| 1-1-X2 | Bank A/c Dr. | | 8,25,000 | |
| | Calls in Advance A/c Dr. | | 60,000 | |
| | Calls in Arrears A/c Dr. | | 15,000 | |
| | To Equity Share Final Call A/c | | | 9,00,000 |
| | (Final call received and adjusted) | | | |
| 1-1-X2 | Interest on Calls in Advance A/c Dr. | | 6,000 | |
| | To Shareholders A/c | | | 6,000 |
| | (Interest on calls in advance) | | | |
| 1-1-X2 | Shareholders A/c Dr. | | 6,000 | |
| | To Bank A/c | | | 6,000 |
| | (Interest paid) | | | |
| 1-3-X2 | Shareholders A/c Dr. | | 250 | |
| | To Interest on Calls in Arrears A/c | | | 250 |
| | (Interest on arrears) | | | |
| 1-3-X2 | Bank A/c Dr. | | 15,250 | |
| | To Calls in Arrears A/c | | | 15,000 |
| | To Shareholders A/c | | | 250 |
| | (Money received for arrears) | | | |

Calculation of Interest on Calls in Advance & Calls in Arrears:
Interest on Calls in Advance = ₹60,000 x 12% x 4/12 = ₹2,400
Interest on Calls in Advance = ₹60,000 x 12% x 6/12 = ₹3,600
Total = ₹6,000
Interest on Calls in Arrears = ₹15,000 x 10% x 2/12 = ₹250

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question: Amar Limited issued 20,000 Equity shares of, 10 each at a premium of 10%, payable ₹ 2 on application; ₹ 4 on allotment (including premium); ₹ 2 on first call and balance on the final call. All the shares were fully subscribed. Mr. M who held 2000 shares paid full remaining amount on first call itself. The final call which was made after 4 months from the first call was fully paid except a shareholder having 200 shares and one another shareholder having 100 shares. They paid their due amount after 3 months and 4 months respectively along with interest on calls in arrears, Company also paid interest on calls in advance to Mr. M. The Company maintains Calls in Arrear and Calls in Advance A/c. Give journal entries to record these transactions. Show workings of Interest calculation.

|          | (Money received on applications for 20,000 shares @ ₹ 2 per share)          |     |              |              |
| 2.       | Equity Share Application A/c Dr.                                            |     | 40,000       |              |

|          | shares for calls in arrears and interest thereupon)                         |     |              |              |

To Interest on Calls in Arrears A/c  
(Being interest on calls in arrears made due at the rate of 10%)  

| 10 |

14 | Bank A/c  

| 310 |

To Calls in Arrears A/c  
To Shareholders A/c  
(Being money received from shareholder having 100 share for calls in arrears and interest thereupon)  

| 300 |  
| 10 |

Calculation of Interest on Calls in Advance & Calls in Arrears:  
Interest on Calls in Advance = ₹ 6,000 x 12% x 4 / 12 = ₹ 240  
Interest on Calls in Arrears ₹ 600 x 10% x 3 / 12 = ₹ 15  
Interest on Calls in Arrears ₹ 300 x 10% x 4 / 12 = ₹ 10  
Table F of The Companies Act,2013 prescribes 10% and 12% p.a. as the maximum rates respectively for calls in arrears and calls in advance.  
Accordingly these rates have been considered while passing the above entries.  
Note: For entry no 9&10, 11&12,13&14 combined entry can also be passed.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question:
The cash book of Mr. Karan shows ₹ 2,60,400 as the balance of bank as on 31st December, 20X2 but you find that it does not agree with the balance as per the bank pass book. On analysis, you found the following discrepancies:

(i) On 15th December, 20X2 the payment side of the cash book was overcast by ₹ 10,000.

(ii) A Cheque for ₹ 1,18,000 issued on 6th December, 20X2 was not taken in the bank Column.

(iii) On 20th December, 20X2 the debit balance of ₹ 8,460 as on the previous day, was brought forward as credit balance in the cash book.

(iv) Of the total cheques amounting to ₹ 12,370 drawn in the last week of December 20X2, cheques aggregating ₹ 9,360 were encashed in December, 20X2.

(v) Dividends of ₹ 35,000 collected by the bank and fire insurance premium of ₹ 7,900 paid by the bank were not recorded in the cash book.

(vi) A Cheque issued to a creditor of ₹ 1,75,000 was recorded twice in the cash book.

(vii) Bill for collection amounting to ₹ 3,000 credited by the bank on 21st December, 20X2 but no advice was received by Mr. Karan till 31st December, 20X2.

(viii) A Customer, who received a cash discount of 3% on his account of ₹ 60,000 paid a cheque on 10th December, 20X2. The cashier erroneously entered the gross amount in the bank column of the cash book.

You are required to prepare the bank reconciliation statement as on 31st December, 20X2.
sub_questions.answer:
| Bank Reconciliation Statement of Mr. Karan as on 31st Dec., 20X2 |
|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| Particulars | Details (₹) | Amount (₹) |
| Balance as per Cash Book | | 2,60,400 |
| Add: Wrong casting in Cash Book | 10,000 | |
| Mistake in bringing forward | 16,920 | |
| Cheques issued but not presented (12,370 - 9,360) | 3,010 | |
| Dividends collected by bank | 35,000 | |
| Cheque recorded twice | 1,75,000 | |
| Bill for collection credited by bank | 53,000 | 2,92,930 |
| Total | | 5,53,330 |
| Less: Cheques issued but not entered in bank | 1,18,000 | |
| Fire insurance premium paid by bank | 7,900 | |
| Discount wrongly entered | 1,800 | (1,27,700) |
| Balance as per Pass Book | | 4,25,630 |

| Adjusted Cash Book |
|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| Particulars | Amount (₹) | Particulars | Amount (₹) |
| To Balance b/d | 2,60,400 | By Cheques not entered | 1,18,000 |
| To Wrong casting | 10,000 | By Fire Insurance Premium | 7,900 |
| To Error in posting | 16,920 | By Discount wrongly entered | 1,800 |
| To Dividends collected | 35,000 | By Balance c/d | 3,69,620 |
| To Cheque recorded twice | 1,75,000 | | |
| Total | 4,97,320 | Total | 4,97,320 |

| Bank Reconciliation Statement |
|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| Particulars | Amount (₹) |
| Balance as per Cash Book (corrected) | 3,69,620 |
| Add: Cheques issued but not presented | 3,010 |
| Add: Bill for collection credited by bank | 53,000 |
| Balance as per Pass Book | 4,25,630 |

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question: On 1st April,2025, Supervisor & Co. which depreciates its machinery @10% p.a. on diminishing balance method, had (₹)29,16,000 to the debit of Machinery Account. On 1st October, 2025, part of machinery purchased on 1st April, 2025 for (₹)40,000 was sold for (₹)1,35,000.  Also, a new machinery at a cost of (₹)4,50,000 was purchased on 1st October, 2025 and installed on the same date and installation charges being (₹)24,000.  The company changed the method of depreciation from diminishing balance method to straight line method which effect from 1st April,2025. The rate of depreciation remains the same.  Show the Machinery Account for the year 2025-26.
sub_questions.answer:
| Date     | Particulars       | Amount (₹) | Date     | Particulars                                      | Amount (₹) |
|----------|-------------------|------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------|------------|
| 01.04.25 | To Balance b/d    | 29,16,000  | 01.10.25 | By Depreciation on machinery sold (W.N.1)        | 9,720      |
| 01.10.25 | To Bank           | 4,74,000   | 01.10.25 | By Bank-Machinery sold                           | 1,35,000   |
|          |                   |            |          | By Loss on sale of machinery (W.N.1)             | 49,680     |
|          |                   |            | 31.03.26 | By Depreciation on remaining machineries (W.N.4) | 2,95,860   |
|          |                   |            | 31.03.26 | By Balance c/d                                   | 28,99,740  |
|          |                   |            |          |                                                  | 33,90,000  |

Working Note:

1. Calculation of amount of Depreciation, written down value and loss on sale of the part of the machinery

| Particulars                                                                 | Amount (₹) |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Cost as on 01.04.2023                                                      | 2,40,000   |
| Less: Depreciation @10% for the year 2023-2024                             | (24,000)   |
| Written Down Value (WDV) as on 31.03.2024 or 01.04.2024                    | 2,16,000   |
| Less: Depreciation @10% for the year 2024-2025                             | (21,600)   |
| Written Down Value (WDV) as on 01.04.2025                                  | 1,94,400   |
| Less: Depreciation @10% for the half year till 30th September, 2025        | (9,720)    |
| Written Down Value (WDV) as on 1.10.2025                                   | 1,84,680   |
| Less: Sale price of the asset sold on 01.10.2025                           | (1,35,000) |
| Loss on sale of Machinery sold                                             | 49,680     |

2. Computation of written down value of the remaining asset as on 01.04.2025

| Particulars                                                                 | Amount (₹) |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Total WDV of the machinery as on 01.04.2025                                | 29,16,000  |
| Less: WDV of the part of the machinery sold as on 01.04.2025(W.N. 1)       | (1,94,400) |
| Written down value of the remaining asset as on 01.04.2025                 | 27,21,600  |

3. Computation of the written down value of the machinery as on 31.03.2026

| Particulars                                                                 | Amount (₹) |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Written down value of the remaining asset as on 01.04.2025                 | 27,21,600  |
| Less: Depreciation @ 10% for the year 2025-2026                            | (2,72,160) |
| Add: New machinery purchased on 1.10.2025 (450,000+24,000)                 | 4,74,000   |
| Less: Depreciation for 6 months @10%                                       | (23,700)   |
| Written down value of the machinery as on 31.03.2026                       | 28,99,740  |

4. Total Depreciation to be charged to Profit and Loss Account during the year 2025-2026

| S.No. | Particulars                                                                 | Depreciation (₹) |
|-------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| 1.    | Depreciation at 10% on existing machinery on 01.04.2025 i.e. (27,21,600*10%)| 2,72,160          |
| 2.    | Depreciation on addition i.e. 474,000*10%*1/2                               | 23,700            |
|       | Sub-Total                                                                   | 2,95,860          |
| 3.    | Depreciation on machinery sold 1.10.2025                                    | 9,720             |
|       | Grand Total                                                                 | 3,05,580          |

Prepare Statement of Affairs as on 31st March, 20X1 and 31st March, 20X2. Find the profit or loss earned by Mr. Sarthak for the year ended 31st March, 20X2.

Mr. Sarthak  
Statement of Affairs as on 31-03-20X1 & 31-03-20X2

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question:
On 1st June, 20X1, Suraj Ltd. issued 43,000 shares of ₹ 100 each payable as follows:  
₹ 20 on application; ₹ 20 on allotment;  
First call of ₹ 30 on 1st Dec, 20X1; and  
Second and final call of ₹ 30 on 1st March, 20X2.  
By 20th July, 40,000 shares were applied for and all applications were accepted. Allotment was made on 1st Aug. All sums due on allotment were received on 15th Sept; those on 1st call were received on 20th Dec.  
You are required to journalise the transactions when accounts were closed on 31st March, 20X2.
sub_questions.answer:
In the books of Suraj Ltd. Journal Entries  

| 20X1 | | Dr. ₹ | Cr. ₹ |

| | (Application money on 40,000 shares at ₹ 20 per share received.) | | |
| Aug 1 | Share Application A/c | Dr. | 8,00,000 |
| | To Share Capital A/c | | 8,00,000 |
| | (The amount transferred to Capital Account on 40,000 shares ₹ 20 on application. Directors’ resolution no......... dated) | | |
| | Share Allotment A/c | Dr. | 8,00,000 |
| | To Share Capital A/c | | 8,00,000 |
| | (Being share allotment made due at 20 per share. Directors’ resolution no.... dated) | | |
| Sept 15 | Bank Account | Dr. | 8,00,000 |
| | To Share Allotment A/c | | 8,00,000 |
| | (The sums due on allotment received.) | | |
| Dec. 1 | Share First Call Account | Dr. | 12,00,000 |
